description_long: This is a squat, round green glass bottle with a wide body and a short, narrow neck, sealed with a light-colored cork stopper. The front of the bottle features a large, weathered parchment-style label with the word 'RUM', a skull and crossbones graphic, and the year '1497' printed in bold, dark letters. The bottle's surface is mostly smooth but shows some subtle scuffs and wear, especially around the label edges, giving it a slightly aged appearance. The glass is opaque and has a glossy finish, reflecting light in several areas. The back and sides of the bottle are plain, with no additional markings or decorations. The overall design suggests it is intended to evoke a vintage or pirate-themed aesthetic, suitable for use as a prop or collectible.
description: A round, green glass bottle with a cork stopper and a weathered 'RUM' label featuring a skull and crossbones. The bottle has a glossy, slightly worn surface and an antique appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the parchment-style 'RUM' label with a skull and crossbones and the year '1497', as well as the cork stopper.
description_view_1: Rear angled view displaying the plain green glass surface and the edge of the label wrapping around the side.
description_view_2: Back view revealing the unadorned, glossy green glass with no visible label or markings.
description_view_3: Side view showing the bottle's rounded shape and a partial glimpse of the label's edge.
attribute_label_or_branding: A large, weathered parchment-style label on the front with the word 'RUM', a black skull and crossbones, and the year '1497' in bold black text.
attribute_damage_or_deformation: Minor surface scuffs and wear around the label edges; no major damage or deformation visible.
